letters.label: Letter One
variants:
Mira,

Your hands were always covered in chalk.

You drew lines on everything.

You said straight lines kept the world honest.

You believed it.

I think I did too.

- Corren

Mira.

You laughed quiet.

Like you were trying not to wake something.

Bram never knew when you were joking.

i did.

- corren

letters.label: Letter Two
variants:
Mira.

The soul-sand was cold.

I do not remember the heat after.

You said something.

I think you said my name.

I think you said Run.
I did not turn around.

- Corren

letters.label: Letter Three
variants:
Bram.

You laughed like falling.

Sudden. Loud. Always too loud.

You said you laughed so the dark would know you were there.

It always did.

- Corren

Bram.

Your shield was too big for you.

You walked sideways through doors.

Esa called it the table.

You said the table never let anyone down.

- Corren

letters.label: Letter Four
variants:
Bram.

The first skull was yours.

You picked it up.

You shouted something.

I could not hear over the fire.

The wheels on this train make three notes.

The first one is yours Bram.

- Corren

Bram.

i wrote this five times.

each time i get to the skull and the pen stops.

you held it up. you grinned.

you said it weighed nothing.

i think it weighed a lot.

The wheels on this train make three notes.

The first one is yours Bram.

- Corren

letters.label: Letter Five
variants:
Esa.

Your hands were cold from brewing.

The rest of you was warm.

Mira said you were the only warm one of us.

She was right.

- Corren

Esa.

You sang while you bandaged.

Old songs. None of us knew them.

You said you made them up as you went.

We pretended to believe you.

- Corren

letters.label: Letter Six
variants:
Esa.

Your hand was on my back.

Cold from brewing.

You pushed once.

I stumbled to safety.

You did not push again.

You were already gone, I think.

Before the push.

- Corren

Esa.

There are people on this train who have lived longer than me.

none of them look right.

i do not want to know what i look like.

you would have told me.

you would have said sit down Corren and let me see.

- corren

letters.label: Letter Seven
variants:
I do not know how I got out.

The portal was broken. I remember that.

I do not remember walking through.

I came up in a field. Tall grass. The train was already there.

It was moving.

It had always been moving. I know that now.

I ran beside it. I jumped for the nearest door.

I do not know how I caught it.

I thought I had made it.

I have been making it for weeks now.

The train does not stop.

There is no off.

- Corren

the heat behind me. the cold ahead.

i did not know which way was up.

i remember falling onto grass.

the grass was not on fire.

that seemed important.

then a building.

i did not know the building.

i know all the buildings in this region.

i mapped this region.

a train was at the building.

the doors were open.

i was alive and the doors were open.

these felt like related facts.

- corren
